headline: Ted Leonsis of Monumental Sports & Entertainment: Taking the regional route to global growth
summary: The chair of the Washington Wizards on brand building, organic growth — and the benefits of having a sovereign wealth fund investor
body_text:
While many sports investors are taking a global approach, picking up assets around the world, Washington DC-based Monumental Sports & Entertainment has adopted a doggedly regional strategy. The company owns ice hockey, basketball and esports franchises, as well as a string of venues, all in and around the US capital.

Founded by former America Online (AOL) president Ted Leonsis in 2010, Monumental has its admirers. In 2023, the Qatar Investment Authority took a minority stake in the business.

Speaking at the Leaders Week sports conference in London earlier this month, he discusses Monumental’s growth strategy, the economics of ownership and the future of cross-border sport with the FT’s sports business correspondent, Samuel Agini.

Samuel Agini: What’s brought you to London?

Ted Leonsis: This Leaders Week conference has emerged as something very important, and the entrepreneurs that started it invited me to speak at their very first conference [in 2008]. I was joking with them. I sai
